zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：弱势震荡下行延续
zh.content:
当前价格持续运行于6.3下方，明显低于SMA20的6.49水平，结构上维持弱势震荡下行格局，短线反弹持续受压

MACD维持-0.12附近且未见明显金叉迹象，动能柱持续为负，均线系统偏空排列，反弹力度不足卖压占优

下方关键支撑关注6.28与6.25区域，若有效跌破可能延伸至6.10一带；上方阻力集中在6.50与6.60区间

短线建议以反弹做空为主，未站稳6.50前不宜追多，需严格设置止损应对区间震荡与假突破风险

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
(AVAX/USD) is currently showing a mild bearish drift, trading around 6.2867 after a 0.44% intraday decline. Price action remains compressed near the lower boundary of the 24-hour range between 6.3174 and 6.2829, suggesting short-term indecision with a slight downward bias. The pair continues to trade below the SMA(20) at 6.49569, indicating that the broader short-term trend remains under pressure from sellers, with limited bullish momentum visible.

Momentum indicators for AVAX reflect weakening conditions as MACD prints -0.12423749, reinforcing bearish momentum in the absence of a confirmed signal line reading. RSI(14) is not available, but price behavior near intraday lows suggests a subdued recovery profile rather than oversold reversal strength. With price still capped below the SMA(20), bullish continuation signals remain unconfirmed and sellers retain short-term control of directional flow.

Key support for AVAX/USD is forming around 6.2829, with a break below potentially exposing 6.20 as the next psychological and structural level. On the upside, immediate resistance sits near 6.3174, aligned with the upper end of the current range, while a stronger barrier emerges at the SMA(20) level of 6.49569. Until price reclaims this moving average, rallies are likely to face selling pressure.

Short-term strategy for Avalanche AVAX/USD favors cautious positioning due to weak momentum and price compression below key moving averages. Traders may consider waiting for a decisive break above 6.32 and sustained recovery toward SMA(20) before initiating long positions. Alternatively, a breakdown below 6.28 could open short opportunities toward 6.20. Risk management remains essential as volatility is low but directional bias leans bearish.
